acip_transport_methods_t(3) Library Functions Manual acip_transport_methods_t(3)

NAME

acip_transport_methods_t - Transport method table (virtual function table).

SYNOPSIS

#include <include/ascii-chat/network/acip/transport.h>

Data Fields


asciichat_error_t(* send )(acip_transport_t *transport, const void *data, size_t len)
Send data through this transport. asciichat_error_t(* recv )(acip_transport_t *transport, void **buffer, size_t *out_len, void **out_allocated_buffer)
Receive data from this transport. asciichat_error_t(* close )(acip_transport_t *transport)
Close this transport. acip_transport_type_t(* get_type )(acip_transport_t *transport)
Get transport type. socket_t(* get_socket )(acip_transport_t *transport)
Get underlying socket (if applicable). bool(* is_connected )(acip_transport_t *transport)
Check if transport is connected. void(* destroy_impl )(acip_transport_t *transport)
Custom destroy implementation (optional).

Detailed Description

Transport method table (virtual function table).

Each transport implementation provides these methods. ACIP protocol code calls these instead of socket functions.

Definition at line 115 of file transport.h.

Field Documentation

asciichat_error_t(* acip_transport_methods_t::close) (acip_transport_t *transport)

Close this transport.

Parameters

transport Transport instance

Returns

ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure

Note

Should be idempotent (safe to call multiple times)

Does NOT free the transport structure itself

Definition at line 156 of file transport.h.

Referenced by acip_transport_destroy().

void(* acip_transport_methods_t::destroy_impl) (acip_transport_t *transport)

Custom destroy implementation (optional).

Parameters

transport Transport instance

Note

Called by acip_transport_destroy() before freeing impl_data

Should free transport-specific resources (peer connections, etc.)

May be NULL if no custom cleanup needed

Definition at line 194 of file transport.h.

Referenced by acip_transport_destroy().

socket_t(* acip_transport_methods_t::get_socket) (acip_transport_t *transport)

Get underlying socket (if applicable).

Parameters

transport Transport instance

Returns

Socket descriptor or INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE

Note

Returns INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE for transports without sockets

Useful for select()/poll() integration

Definition at line 175 of file transport.h.

Referenced by acip_client_receive_and_dispatch(), and acip_server_receive_and_dispatch().

acip_transport_type_t(* acip_transport_methods_t::get_type) (acip_transport_t *transport)

Get transport type.

Parameters

transport Transport instance

Returns

Transport type enum value

Definition at line 164 of file transport.h.

Referenced by acip_transport_destroy().

bool(* acip_transport_methods_t::is_connected) (acip_transport_t *transport)

Check if transport is connected.

Parameters

transport Transport instance

Returns

true if connected, false otherwise

Definition at line 183 of file transport.h.

Referenced by acip_client_receive_and_dispatch(), acip_server_receive_and_dispatch(), and acip_transport_destroy().

asciichat_error_t(* acip_transport_methods_t::recv) (acip_transport_t *transport, void **buffer, size_t *out_len, void **out_allocated_buffer)

Receive data from this transport.

Parameters

transport Transport instance
buffer Output buffer (allocated by transport)
out_len Received data length
out_allocated_buffer Buffer to free (may differ from buffer)

Returns

ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure

Note

Allocates buffer, caller must free via out_allocated_buffer

May block until data arrives

For TCP: calls socket_recv_all()

For WebSocket: unwraps WebSocket frame

Definition at line 145 of file transport.h.

Referenced by acip_client_receive_and_dispatch(), acip_server_receive_and_dispatch(), and client_receive_thread().

asciichat_error_t(* acip_transport_methods_t::send) (acip_transport_t *transport, const void *data, size_t len)

Send data through this transport.

Parameters

transport Transport instance
data Data to send (NOT owned by transport)
len Data length in bytes

Returns

ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure

Note

Does NOT take ownership of data buffer

May block until send completes

For TCP: calls socket_send_all()

For WebSocket: wraps in WebSocket frame

Definition at line 129 of file transport.h.
